meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve no version control (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve Version Control
content.seo_description: Learn how to use an Antigravity project template to overcome no version control. A Beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for students.
content.answer_block: The absence of version control is a ticking time bomb for your portfolio. By implementing an Antigravity project template, you automate repository initialization and branching logic. This setup enforces professional workflows, ensuring your code remains modular, recoverable, and ready for deployment while building the habits of a senior software engineer.
content.intro:
You’ve spent hours crafting the perfect logic, only for a rogue 'delete' or a corrupted file to erase your progress. It is a rite of passage for every student, but it is also the fastest way to kill your momentum. Without version control, you aren't just writing code; you are building on shifting sand.

Enter the Antigravity project template. This isn't just a folder structure; it is an architectural safeguard. By integrating this with Claude Code, you transform your local environment into a professional-grade development hub that tracks every iteration, protects your history, and allows you to experiment without fear of catastrophic loss.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Establish the root directory with the Antigravity template to ensure standardized file organization and initial git tracking.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a new project structure using the Antigravity standard template, ensuring a .gitignore, README.md, and src directory are initialized.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize project using Antigravity template'

steps.title: Configure Atomic Commits
steps.description: Set up your environment to handle small, logical changes rather than massive, unreadable dumps of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the project hooks to enforce commit message linting and atomic change tracking.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Enable strict commit message linting and atomic commit hooks'

steps.title: Enable Automated Version Tracking
steps.description: Use Claude Code to monitor file modifications and suggest commit points, removing the mental load of manual tracking.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a background watcher that monitors src changes and suggests commit messages based on logic diffs.

steps.title: Establish Branching Strategy
steps.description: Create a feature-branch workflow to isolate experimental features from your stable codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a script to automate the creation of feature branches and merging workflows within the Antigravity structure.

steps.title: Finalize Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Connect your version-controlled repo to a cloud provider to ensure your project is live and verifiable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment configuration file that integrates with the existing version history to automate builds.

pro_tips: Always commit before running an AI-suggested refactor so you can roll back instantly if the logic breaks.
Use descriptive branch names like 'feat/auth-module' instead of generic names to keep your history readable for recruiters.
Treat your '.gitignore' as a security document; never commit environment variables or local API keys.
Review the 'git diff' output manually after using Claude Code to understand exactly how the AI modified your logic.
Implement a 'checkpoint' commit every time you reach a functional milestone to maintain a clear roadmap of your project's evolution.
resources: Git Best Practices for Students: https://git-scm.com/doc
content.next_steps: Once your template is live, push your repository to GitHub, document your process in a README, and start building your second feature branch.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are an engineer. Stop losing progress and start shipping professional software. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
